title: betula latifolia var. pluricostata
intro: Betula latifolia var. pluricostata, also known as the narrow-leaved birch, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. Its bark is rich in salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it a valuable resource for medicinal uses. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the bark of Betula latifolia var. pluricostata is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
